target.title: fix(sessions): defer daily rollover during active reply runs
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Fixes #96546.

When an inbound message arrives after the daily reset boundary while the previous turn is still active, auto-reply session initialization can mark the existing session entry stale before active-run admission runs. Before this patch, that implicit rollover path could create a replacement session and archive the old transcript while the active run was still able to append to it.

## Why This Change Was Made
The initializer now detects the narrow case where an implicit stale rollover targets the same session id that is currently registered as an active reply run. In that case it reuses the existing entry for this turn, preserving the live transcript path and store reference. Explicit `/new` and `/reset` behavior stays unchanged because the guard only applies when no reset command triggered the rollover.

The persistent session-store shape is unchanged. This does not add, remove, or rename serialized fields; it only changes whether an existing `{ sessionId, sessionStartedAt, updatedAt }` entry is reused during an in-memory active-run conflict. Existing stores need no migration, and the regression test seeds an old-format store entry directly to prove compatibility.

## User Impact
A long-running turn that crosses the daily reset boundary is no longer split across an archived transcript and a recreated orphan session when another inbound message arrives. The session remains attached to the live transcript while the active-run admission path handles queueing, steering, or follow-up behavior.

Operators still keep the daily reset behavior for inactive sessions and explicit reset commands.

## Evidence
Head commit: `f733322353e9f0b5f3cc8be67c5ab6e9646016d2`.

Local targeted behavior proof:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts` -> 1 file passed, 120 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/reply-turn-admission.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run-queue.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts` -> 2 Vitest shards passed: gateway shard 2 files/20 tests, auto-reply shard 2 files/13 tests.
- `pnpm oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.tool-result-persist-hook.test.ts` -> all 3 touched files formatted.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` -> passed.
- `git diff --check` -> no whitespace errors.

Behavior observed in the regression test:

- Seeds a persisted session store entry with `sessionId`, `sessionStartedAt`, and `updatedAt` from before the daily reset boundary.
- Creates the matching transcript file and registers the same `sessionKey`/`sessionId` as an active reply run.
- Calls `initSessionState` after the daily reset boundary with no explicit reset command.
- Verifies `isNewSession === false`, `resetTriggered === false`, and the same `sessionId` is returned.
- Verifies the transcript file remains at the original path with its content intact.
- Verifies no `.jsonl.reset.*` archive is created.
- Verifies the persisted store still points at the original `sessionId` and original `sessionStartedAt`.
target.author: lin-hongkuan
